title: Extra-Long Buffet Cabinet with Drawers
summary: A near-8-foot plywood buffet — three drawers and two doors behind a separately-built face frame, a planked solid-wood top, and shelving on either side.
description:
This buffet is a genuinely large plywood carcass — top, bottom, sides, and two internal dividers form four bays across nearly 8 feet — so the source's own advice is worth repeating: take your time, use clamps, use glue, and check the outside corners' diagonals for square before anything is permanent. A project this size amplifies a small out-of-square error across its whole length in a way a nightstand-sized carcass never would.

Build the face frame as its own separate, flat assembly before attaching it to the front of the cabinet — the same discipline this catalog's smaller face-framed pieces use, just at a larger scale where it matters even more. The top is solid-wood planking rather than plywood, laid over the carcass and either nailed down (visible heads) or screwed from underneath (hidden), giving the whole piece a furniture-grade surface rather than a sheet-good edge.

The three drawers are sized to the actual cabinet opening and your specific drawer slides' clearance requirements, not to a fixed universal number — build them to fit inside the cabinet, inset 3/4" for the drawer faces, before finalizing drawer face dimensions. The two end doors are simple frame-and-panel construction, a 1x4 frame around a 1/2" plywood inset panel, hung on either inset or surface-mount exposed hinges with a magnetic catch to keep them closed.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood into carcass strips.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood or hardboard, for the back

cutList.material: 1/4" plywood or hardboard, for the back

cutList.note: Measure your slide clearance before final cut.

cutList.material: 1/4" plywood or hardboard, for the back

cutList.note: Sized to your actual door openings — measure and cut to fit.
Sized to fit your assembled door frames.

steps.title: Build the plywood carcass
steps.body: Build the basic frame of top, bottom, sides, and dividers with pocket holes and glue. Take your time and use clamps — this is a much bigger task than it looks given the project's overall size. Adjust for square by comparing diagonal measurements at the outside corners until they match.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: While the carcass is square, attach the back with glue and 3/4" nails or staples.
materials: 1/4" plywood or hardboard, for the back

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame as its own separate, flat assembly, then attach it to the front of the cabinet with 1-1/4" nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the top planking
steps.body: Cover the top with planking, nailing and gluing down with 1-1/4" nails (heads will show) or screwing from underneath to hide fasteners.

steps.title: Add shelves and moulding
steps.body: Add the shelves fixed or with a shelf-pin jig for adjustability. Apply base moulding around the bottom and top edge moulding to the top edge.

steps.title: Build and install the drawers
steps.body: Build the drawers to fit inside the cabinet and your specific drawer slides. Install inset 3/4" to allow room for the drawer faces. Attach the faces with 1-1/4" nails and glue, avoiding hardware locations, then pull each drawer out and add a few screws from the back for extra hold.

steps.title: Build and hang the doors
steps.body: Build simple frame-and-panel doors to fit the end openings — a 1x4 frame around a 1/2" plywood inset panel. Attach with inset or surface-mount hinges, and add magnetic catches to keep them closed. Sand and finish the whole piece.

images.alt: Extra-long buffet cabinet with drawers and doors
